commit.message:
SQL GROUP BY: implement true per-group aggregation with multi-column key support (#1348)

* Initial plan

* feat(sql): implement true GROUP BY per-group aggregation and multi-column support

- Add _build_group_by_tensors() helper in convert.py that uses ONNX Unique
  to compute inverse_indices (group index per row), first_indices (first
  occurrence index per group), n_groups, and an updated col_map that maps
  GROUP BY key columns to their unique-value tensors.
  Single-column GROUP BY uses Unique directly; multi-column GROUP BY casts
  all key columns to float64, stacks them into a 2-D matrix, and uses
  Unique with axis=0.

- Extend _ExprEmitter in _expr.py with three new optional parameters
  (group_by_inverse_indices, group_by_first_indices, group_by_n_groups)
  and a new _emit_grouped_agg() method that uses ScatterElements for
  per-group SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX/COUNT aggregations.

- Update _populate_graph() to call _build_group_by_tensors() and wire
  GROUP BY info into _ExprEmitter when a GROUP BY clause is present.

- Update test_sql_convert.py: fix existing GROUP BY tests to reflect
  true per-group output and add tests for AVG, MIN, MAX, key-in-SELECT,
  2-column GROUP BY, 3-column GROUP BY, and key-in-SELECT with 2 columns.

commit.message:
fix: prevent wheel scroll-down from resetting _userScrolledAway during streaming

Touchpad/precision-scroll devices fire interleaved up+down deltas in
the same gesture. Combined with the timing of programmatic ScrollToEnd,
a wheel-down event could arrive with distanceFromBottom=0 (because
ScrollToEnd just ran), immediately resetting _userScrolledAway=false
and undoing the user's scroll-up intent.

Remove the false-resetting path from OnUserWheelScroll entirely.
Re-enabling auto-scroll is now only handled by:
- ResetAutoScroll() when user sends a message
- OnScrollChanged detecting scrollbar drag to within 8px of bottom

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix SDK UserInputHandler targeting wrong chat in parallel sessions

The native UserInputHandler (SDK's ask_question path) was capturing
CurrentChat?.Id at execution time instead of using the chat.Id from
the enclosing EnsureSessionAsync scope. When two chats run in parallel
and the user switches away, the question would either appear in the
wrong chat or silently target the wrong chat's messages.

Capture chat.Id in a closure variable (inputHandlerChatId) at handler
creation time, matching the pattern already used by BuildAskQuestionTool
and BuildBrowserTools.

commit.message:
fix: prevent BringIntoView from overriding user scroll position

StrataThink.RequestExpandedReveal() called BringIntoView() whenever
IsExpanded changed to true — including programmatic changes from data
bindings during streaming. This bypassed StrataChatShell's
_userScrolledAway flag entirely, pulling the viewport to the bottom
every time a tool call expanded.

Now track whether expansion was triggered by user interaction (click
or keyboard) vs programmatic binding. Only call BringIntoView for
user-initiated expansions.

Also fix OnDetachedFromVisualTree to use RemoveHandler for
PointerWheelChangedEvent (matching AddHandler registration).

commit.message:
Fix scroll threshold: check offset from top, not overflow

The previous 200px overflow threshold was too small — with a typical
user message (~150px) plus padding, the scroll offset from the top
can easily reach 300+ px even with just 1-2 tool calls.

Now checks offset <= 350 (how far the user message is scrolled above
the viewport) instead of overflow <= 200. Also guards against adjusting
when the user manually scrolled away, and skips for actively streaming
chats to avoid fighting with auto-scroll.

commit.message:
Add `ReadOnlySpan<byte>` overloads for JNI member lookups (#1393)

Add UTF-8 span overloads for JNI class, method, and field lookups
that accept ReadOnlySpan<byte> parameters. Callers can pass u8 string
literals (e.g., "java/lang/Object"u8) to avoid string-to-UTF-8
marshalling overhead entirely.

New overloads (function pointers backend only):
- JniEnvironment.Types.FindClass/TryFindClass(ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.InstanceMethods.GetMethodID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.StaticMethods.GetStaticMethodID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.InstanceFields.GetFieldID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniEnvironment.StaticFields.GetStaticFieldID(…, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<byte>)
- JniType constructor, Get*/GetCached* for methods, fields, constructors

Contributes to #950

* Add tests for ReadOnlySpan<byte> JNI lookup overloads

Tests cover:
- Equivalence: string vs u8 overloads return same JNI IDs
- End-to-end: constructor + method invocation via u8 path
- Cached lookups: GetCached* returns same instance
- Error handling: disposed type, class not found, invalid signature

* Remove Class.forName fallback from UTF-8 FindClass overload

The Class.forName fallback requires converting the UTF-8 span back to
a managed string and then to a Java string, which negates the
zero-alloc benefit of the UTF-8 path. Callers who need the classloader
fallback can use the existing string-based FindClass overload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix hover styles targeting Button instead of PART_Surface

Hover styles that set Background on the Button control (instead of the
template's Border#PART_Surface) are silently overridden by the default
Button ControlTheme hover, which directly targets PART_Surface. This
causes ControlHover to show instead of the intended hover color.

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive self-healing for multi-agent groups lost on restart (#412)

## Problem
Multi-agent groups were silently lost across app restarts. The
`organization.json` on disk had:
- All groups with `IsMultiAgent=false`
- All sessions with `Role=Worker` (even orchestrator sessions)
- Multi-agent group IDs from event-diagnostics didn't exist in the file

Affected teams: PR Review Squad, MAUI - PR Reviewer, FixWeirdInfoPList,
PP- Organize, Evaluate Ortinau Skills, Implement & Challenge (6 teams
total, ~25 sessions).

## Root Cause
The self-healing in `LoadOrganization` only checked `Role ==
Orchestrator`, but all sessions defaulted to `Worker` when the org file
was stale. Chicken-and-egg: can't detect orchestrator → can't heal group
→ can't protect sessions → sessions scatter.

## Fix
Three-phase self-healing in `HealMultiAgentGroups()`:

1. **Phase 1**: Detect orchestrator sessions by name pattern
(`*-orchestrator*`) and restore `Role=Orchestrator`
2. **Phase 2**: Restore `IsMultiAgent` on groups that have orchestrator
sessions (with name matching to avoid incorrectly marking repo groups)
3. **Phase 3**: Reconstruct missing multi-agent groups from scattered
sessions by detecting team members via name prefix patterns

Also adds save verification and error logging to
`event-diagnostics.log`.

## Tests
6 new tests covering:
- Orchestrator role detection by name pattern (with suffix variants)
- Group reconstruction from scattered sessions
- Multiple scattered teams handling
- No false positives on regular session names
- No-op when groups are already correct

All 55 organization tests pass.

commit.message:
Add steps action, quiet mode, and compact snapshots for token efficiency

Three optimizations targeting the remaining ~60% token waste from
auto-snapshots attached to every browser action:

1. steps action: Execute multiple actions in one call with a single
   snapshot at the end. Calendar navigation (click next month x2 +
   click day) goes from 3 tool calls + 3 snapshots to 1 call + 1
   snapshot. Max 20 steps per call. Supports all action types except
   nested steps/download/wait.

2. quiet mode: Append ' quiet' to target or set value='quiet' on
   click/press/scroll/clear/back to suppress the auto-snapshot
   entirely. For when the LLM already knows the next action.

3. Compact snapshots: Reduce element cap from 80 to 50 and text
   preview from 3000 to 1500 chars in LookAsync. Cuts ~40% of
   snapshot tokens without losing important information.

Also updates tool descriptions and system prompt with efficiency
best practices emphasizing steps and quiet mode.

commit.message:
Fix iOS TestFlight build: remove hardcoded signing identity, clean up plist injection (#411)

## Problem
The iOS build has been failing on TestFlight CI ([run
#23331578098](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/23331578098/job/67864110605))
with:

```
Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8): no identity found
error MSB3073: codesign --force --sign "Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8)" ... exited with code 1.
```

## Root Cause
The csproj had a post-build target (`InjectIOSPrivacyKeys`) with a
**hardcoded codesign exec** using Jakub Florkowski's signing identity.
This was part of a workaround that re-injected NS*UsageDescription
privacy keys into Info.plist after build, then re-signed the app.

## Fix
**Removed the entire PlistBuddy injection mechanism** (both
`InjectMacCatalystPrivacyKeys` and `InjectIOSPrivacyKeys` targets + the
hardcoded codesign).

The injection was a workaround for an old MAUI behavior that stripped
`NS*UsageDescription` keys from the output Info.plist. This is no longer
the case — verified with a fully clean build (`rm -rf bin/ obj/`) on
.NET 10 that the `CompileAppManifest` task in
[`dotnet/macios`](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs)
now preserves all source plist keys.

The privacy keys (`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`,
`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`) are already defined in both
`Platforms/iOS/Info.plist` and `Platforms/MacCatalyst/Info.plist` and
survive the build pipeline untouched.

## Verification
- ✅ Full clean build (bin/ + obj/ nuked) on Mac Catalyst — 0 errors
- ✅ Privacy keys present in output `.app/Contents/Info.plist` without
any injection
- ✅ Confirmed `CompileAppManifest` [source
code](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs#L105)
reads full source plist and doesn't strip NS* keys

---------

commit.message:
Drastically improve browser form automation for React/Vue/Angular sites

Major overhaul of browser automation tools based on analysis of a real
chat session where booking restaurant reservations failed due to
framework-incompatible form interaction (1131 tool calls, 282 reasoning
blocks, most reservations failed).

Core fix - Framework-aware type action:
- Replace naive el.value=text with native setter trick used by
  Playwright and React Testing Library
- Use Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set
  to bypass React/Vue/Angular value tracking
- Reset el._valueTracker so React detects the change
- Dispatch InputEvent (not just Event) with proper data/inputType fields
- Add blur event dispatch to trigger form validation
- Handle both input and textarea elements with respective prototype setters

New browser_do actions:
- fill: Batch fill multiple form fields in one call via JSON mapping,
  eliminating per-field snapshots that wasted enormous context tokens
- read_form: Read all form fields with names, values, types, required
  status, validation errors, and element indices in a single call
- clear: Clear a field using the framework-aware approach

Improved existing actions:
- select: Now handles custom dropdown components (react-select, MUI,
  downshift) by clicking to open then clicking the matching option,
  not just native <select> elements. Uses async IIFE for proper
  Promise handling.
- browser_js: Wrap user scripts in try/catch, return structured error
  messages instead of silently returning null. Handle undefined/null
  returns explicitly. Support Promise-returning scripts.

System prompt updates:
- Document all new actions with examples
- Add form filling best practices (read_form -> fill -> verify)
- Guide LLM to use fill for batch operations instead of per-field type
- Advise moving on quickly from CAPTCHA/CC-blocked platforms

fix: register PointerWheelChanged with handledEventsToo to detect user scroll

The ScrollViewer handles PointerWheelChanged and sets Handled=true,
which prevented the += subscription from firing. This meant wheel
scroll-up during active streaming was not detected, allowing
auto-scroll to override the user's manual scroll position.

commit.message:
feat: migrate embedded frontend from custom MVX framework to React 18

Replace the custom MVX rendering framework (BaseComponent, StateStore,
Compositor, MVX orchestrator) with React 18 functional components using
an adapter pattern that preserves the existing public API.

## What changed

- **Adapter pattern**: ReactCaptureMenu and ReactResultView implement the
  exact same public API as the original classes, using ExternalStore +
  useSyncExternalStore for state management instead of MVX state IDs
- **32 React components** replace 31 MVX components across CaptureMenu
  (5 components) and ResultView (18 components + JSONRenderTree)
- **Visual regression tests**: 38 Playwright screenshot tests ensure
  pixel-level visual parity with the original UI
- **SCSS unchanged**: Same 3 SCSS files, same class names, same DOM
  structure — zero CSS changes needed
- **Ace editor**: Wrapped with useRef/useEffect for proper lifecycle
  management (no StrictMode — incompatible with Ace)

## Architecture

- ExternalStore<T> bridges imperative API → React reactivity
- Adapter classes translate method calls → store.setState() → re-render
- React components are purely presentational (read store, call handlers)
- Context pattern passes adapter instance to component tree
- JSONRenderItem discriminated union for recursive JSON tree rendering

## Bug fixes

- hide() now actually works (MVX had truthy-check bug)
- Removed dead IE11 navigator.msSaveBlob code path
- Fixed falsy number rendering in VisualStateListItem (textureLayer=0)

## Bundle impact

- Before: 667 KB (MVX + Ace)
- After: 794 KB (React + ReactDOM + Ace) — +127 KB for React
